'''Adam O'Brien''' (born 11 July 1993) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He has previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], spending time away from [[Odsal Stadium|Odsal]] with the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in 2013. In 2017 he was sent on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] to [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
O'Brien was born in [[Halifax, West Yorkshire]], England.

Playing for King Cross Park RLFC a Halifax based community club as a junior which he is still heavily involved in. He joined Bradford Junior Development system and was called up to the [[England Knights]] in 2019 along with 3 other Giants players.<ref>https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/huddersfield-giants/huddersfield-giants-quartet-are-selected-for-england-knights-squad-1-9674104 {{Dead link|date=February 2022}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Bradford Bulls===
O'Brien featured in the pre-season game against [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]]. O'Brien was not selected in any league or cup matches this season. At the end of the 2010 season he was given a three-year professional contract.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

O'Brien featured in three of the four pre-season friendlies. He played against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]], Dewsbury and the [[Keighley Cougars]]. O'Brien made his [[Super League]] début against [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], however he was not selected until later against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and played until the match against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]. He did feature in the [[Challenge Cup]] game against [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

O'Brien featured in three of the four pre-season friendlies. He played against Castleford, Dewsbury and Hull F.C. O'Brien featured in Round 19 ([[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]]) to Round 21 ([[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]).
He signed a five-year extension to his contract midway through the season.{{citation needed|date=June 2017}}

O'Brien featured in the pre-season friendlies against Dewsbury and Leeds. He featured in Round 5 against Huddersfield and then in Round 7 against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. O'Brien also featured in Round 10 against [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] and Round 17 against Hull Kingston Rovers. His next appearance was in Round 25 against Castleford to Round 27 Huddersfield. O'Brien featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] against the [[Rochdale Hornets]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

O'Brien featured in the pre-season games against Hull F.C. and Castleford. 
[[File:Adam O'Brien.jpg|thumb|right|O'Brien playing for the Bradford Bulls in 2014]]
He featured in Round 1 Castleford to Round 14 [[Catalans Dragons]] then in Round 16 against Salford to Round 27 [[London Broncos]]. O'Brien featured in Round 4 ([[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]) to the Quarter Final against [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[Challenge Cup]].{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

O'Brien re-signed with Bradford for another two years despite their relegation to the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for the 2015 season.

O'Brien featured in the pre-season friendlies against Castleford and Leeds. He featured in Round 1 [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] to Round 23 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). O'Brien played in Qualifier 1 against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] to Qualifier 6 ([[Leigh Centurions]]). O'Brien played in the £1 Million Game against Wakefield Trinity. He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 [[Workington Town]] to Round 5 Hull Kingston Rovers. At the end of the 2015 season O'Brien won the T&A player of the year, the Bradford members player of the year and Bradford Players' player of the year.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

O'Brien featured in both pre-season friendlies against Leeds and Castleford. He featured in Round 1 [[Featherstone Rovers]] to Round 22 [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]. Adam played in the Championship Shield Game 1 [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] to the Final [[Sheffield Eagles]]. Midway through the season O'Brien signed a contract extension keeping him at the club until the end of 2019.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Dewsbury Rams===
O'Brien was dual registered with [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]] for the 2013 season. He featured against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]], [[Featherstone Rovers]], [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] (two spells), [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] (two spells), [[York City Knights]] (two spells), [[Workington Town]] (two spells), [[Sheffield Eagles]] (two spells), [[Keighley Cougars|Keighley]] (two spells), [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (two spells) and [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]]. He scored against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (2 tries), [[York City Knights]] (2 tries), [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] (1 try), [[Sheffield Eagles]] (1 try) and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] (1 try).{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Huddersfield Giants===
Following Bradford's liquidation prior to the 2017 season, O'Brien signed a three-year deal following teammate [[Alex Mellor]] to [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/38542202|title=Adam O'Brien: Huddersfield Giants sign Bradford Bulls hooker|work=BBC Sport|date=7 January 2017|accessdate =31 January 2017}}</ref> In the 2018 Super League Season Adam O'Brien received the [[Huddersfield Giants]] Players Player of The Year Award. In January 2019, O'Brien signed a new three-year deal until the end of 2022.
[[File:Adam O'Brien Huddersfield.jpg|thumb|right|300px|O'Brien playing for the Huddersfield Giants in 2019]]

===Halifax Panthers===
On 5 October 2023, it was reported that he had signed a 3 year permanent deal with his hometown club [[Halifax Panthers|Halifax]] commencing the 2024 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=5 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Ex-Huddersfield Giants hooker makes Championship switch: 'I'm absolutely buzzing to be back at my hometown club'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ex-huddersfield-giants-hooker-makes-championship-switch-im-absolutely-buzzing-to-be-back-at-my-hometown-club}}</ref>

==Senior international career==

===England Academy===
O'Brien appeared as an interchange in a victory against France U18 in June 2010 and in both tests against the Australian Schoolboys in December 2010, England Academy won the series 2–0 with both games being played at [[Leigh Sports Village]].{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}

O'Brien is the younger brother of the former [[Association football|footballer]], [[Luke O'Brien]].{{citation needed|date=January 2020}}
